title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pachyphylla
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pachyphylla, also known as kidney vetch, is a subspecies of perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and promote tissue repair. The health benefits of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pachyphylla include its ability to support kidney function, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including urinary tract infections and skin irritations.
